items.title: Officer accuses disabled driver of holding phone with missing hand
items.summary: A traffic stop in Florida has gone viral after an officer accused a disabled driver of holding a phone in her right hand

items.title: Ukraine drones strike Russian oil facility, as Kyiv and Moscow trade blows
items.summary: Strikes have seen 13,000 residents without power in Zaporizhzhia as Russia and Ukraine trade drone attacks.

items.title: Voting ongoing in snap elections in Malta, governing party expected to win
items.summary: Malta's economy to dominate this year’s election, with rising rents and infrastructure at the forefront of debates.

items.summary: Senegal arrive for their third consecutive World Cup as arguably the best side in Africa - and aim to lift the trophy.

items.summary: Trump weighs next steps on Iran deal as Tehran insists negotiations are continuing and no final agreement exists.

items.title: Satire, social media and India’s Gen Z revolt
items.summary: A satirical online movement challenges power and propaganda in India.

items.title: Barcelona sign England winger Anthony Gordon from Newcastle
items.summary: Gordon netted 17 goals for Newcastle in all competitions this season, including 10 in 12 Champions League games.

items.title: Rwanda-Russia nuclear deal underscores Africa’s shifting power balance
items.summary: Rwanda turns to Russia for nuclear training and research, advancing ambitions in technology, healthcare and diplomacy.

items.title: Frustration in Lebanon as Israel expands invasion

items.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass congressional map favouring Republicans
items.summary: Louisiana approves new congressional map eliminating a majority-Black district after an April Supreme Court ruling.

items.title: First survivor rescued from flooded cave in Laos

items.summary: Israel pushes deeper into Lebanon just days after Israel's prime minister ordered 70 percent of Gaza to be occupied.

items.title: Trump pledges to withdraw from Kennedy Center after court strikes his name
items.summary: Judge Christopher Cooper ruled against Trump's bid to add his name to the building and close the theatre for two years.

items.title: ICE agent arrested over shooting of Venezuelan man in US immigration raid
items.summary: The charges stem from the January 14 shooting of Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis in Minneapolis during Operation Metro Surge.

items.summary: Lula warned that labelling Brazil's criminal networks as 'terrorists' could 'undermine' local law enforcement efforts.

items.title: Ebola treatment centre rebuilt after being torched by protesters in DRC
items.summary: Workers in eastern DR Congo are rebuilding an Ebola treatment centre that was burned by protesters earlier this month.

items.title: UN: “100% of Gaza” should be for Palestinians
items.summary: United Nations spokesperson Stephane Dujarric said “100% of Gaza” should belong to Palestinians.

items.summary: Ukrainian leader's move to rename army unit after fighters who killed Poles in WWII causes 'outrage' in Poland.

items.summary: Canada strengthens Chinese trade ties, seeking economic diversification amid growing US–Canada trade tensions

items.summary: Bondi, who was fired in April, defended the release of Epstein records in a closed-door congressional hearing.

items.title: Israel, Russia among new additions on UN sexual violence ‘blacklist’
